guardrails.title: API route protection inventory must stay current
guardrails.risk: Sensitive/public endpoint changes can drift without governance visibility.
guardrails.notes: Controller inventory and status model are validated against source controllers and explicit allowlist.

guardrails.title: Development/demo endpoints must remain environment-gated
guardrails.risk: Development or demo routes can be exposed in production.
guardrails.notes: Suspicious controller names require IsDevelopment or explicit allowlist reason.

guardrails.title: High-confidence secret logging patterns are blocked
guardrails.risk: Credentials and secret material can leak through logs/source.
guardrails.notes: High-confidence logger/console patterns are blocked; safe policy docs and sanitizers are excluded.

guardrails.title: Authentication defaults preserve compatibility and avoid secret hardcoding
guardrails.risk: Accidental forced-login rollout or leaked static API keys in config.
guardrails.notes: Checks appsettings defaults and authentication boundary documentation consistency.

guardrails.title: Rate-limiting defaults remain compatibility-safe and key-safe
guardrails.risk: Accidental throttling rollout or raw API key use in limiter partitioning.
guardrails.notes: Validates defaults, non-claims, and fingerprint-vs-raw-key behavior.

guardrails.title: Audit metadata sanitization and payload-safe event registry are enforced
guardrails.risk: Audit trail can leak secret or full payload data.
guardrails.notes: Checks sanitizer behavior and registry allowPayload policy.

guardrails.title: InMemory provider production risk must be explicit and controlled
guardrails.risk: Accidental production reliance on non-durable providers.
guardrails.notes: Allows transitional InMemory defaults only when explicitly documented as non-durable in security inventory.

guardrails.title: False security/compliance claims are blocked in docs
guardrails.risk: Overclaiming security/compliance can mislead users and reviewers.
guardrails.notes: Forbidden positive claims are blocked outside non-claim/future/roadmap contexts.

guardrails.title: Frontend source must not hardcode real tokens/API keys
guardrails.risk: Client-side secret material leakage and insecure token handling.
guardrails.notes: High-confidence hardcoded token/key patterns are blocked in frontend source.

nonClaims: No full multi-tenant isolation claim yet.
No external identity provider integration claim.
No claim that all API endpoints are protected yet.
